response.justification: The candidate brings genuine geospatial domain experience (founder of the GISCollective geo-data platform, Green Map GIS work, FOSS4G speaker) and proven low-latency systems engineering in DLang at Sociomantic, handling millions of requests per second with async I/O and caching. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ or Rust: his commercial C++ exposure is a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a thesis project, and he lists no Rust. He also has no direct routing, navigation guidance, or map-tile pipeline experience, and his recent years have been TypeScript/Node product work rather than systems programming, so an interview would need to probe whether the systems skills transfer.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial experience: founded and operates an open-source geo-data platform used by Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput backend work in DLang (async I/O, fibers, caching) at millions of requests/sec
Strong engineering rigor: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and author of testing tools and workshops
concerns: Modern C++ experience is brief (~7 months commercial, 2014-2015) and dated; no Rust experience listed
No navigation guidance, routing, or map-tile pipeline background, which the role explicitly requires
Recent roles (2020-present) are TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, a drift away from systems-level engineering
